danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring avoidance or engagement with rattle weapon
Cumulative damage taken; loss of all health results in death
Primary antagonist; ultimate source of kingdom threat; life force drain mechanic

danger_signals.visual_form: Absence of babies in kingdom (story context)
danger_signals.meaning: World state corruption; Upa is exception chosen by fairy for rattle weapon

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items dropped from special blocks with Upa's face
safety_signals.meaning: Health replenishment; reduces immediate danger state
Temporary maximum health increase for current world; enhanced survivability

safety_signals.visual_form: Player weapon; distinguishes Upa from other babies
safety_signals.meaning: Protection mechanism granted by trapped fairy; enables enemy inflation mechanic
Temporary invulnerability state and run speed boost

safety_signals.visual_form: Hourglasses collected grant temporary enemy freeze
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical safety window; allows navigation past threats

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Magical environments; milk-based health items; fairy presence; cake-themed level geometry

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Generic hostile creatures influenced by Zai's life-force drain
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemies throughout levels; kidnapped babies as absence marker; corrupted kingdom state

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Magical rattle (granted by trapped fairy from urn)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Rattle as primary tool; appears in narrative as protective force

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Adults stripped of life force; all babies kidnapped except Upa
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies spawn and patrol levels; world requires progression through stages to restore
Single hit from enemy causes death; increased enemy aggression perception
Upa gains run speed; enemies cannot damage player; temporary state ends after duration
Inflated enemy becomes platform or projectile; must not stay on platform too long or it explodes; can be pushed as weapon

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage clear indicator; world clear music plays
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Progression to next world; difficulty or enemy complexity increases
Game reset; return to title screen

environmental_storytelling.example: Kidnapped babies are not visually present in the kingdom; their absence communicates Zai's curse and establishes player's unique exemption
Milk bottles and hearts hidden in Upa-faced blocks communicate safe resource locations within hostile environment
Cake-themed stage design reflects whimsical, child-centric world; contrasts with corrupted enemy placement
Baby protagonist with magical rattle distinguishes Upa as chosen/special; rattle is visible marker of fairy protection
Different hostile creatures throughout levels suggest ecosystem corrupted by Zai's life-force drain; variety indicates spread of corruption

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Crawls; uses rattle to inflate enemies; collects items; navigates platforms
Gains ability to run; enemies cannot damage; temporary state
Health meter decreases; visual/audio feedback on hit
Patrol level; attack on contact; take damage from rattle
Float upward; become temporary platform; time limit before explosion; can be pushed as projectile
Cease movement; remain vulnerable; temporary state
Primary antagonist; represents ultimate threat; story culmination encounter

notes: Bio Miracle Bokutte Upa communicates world-state primarily through narrative framing (kidnapped babies, corrupted kingdom) and enemy/item presence. Danger signals rely on health meter visibility and enemy spawning patterns. Safety is communicated through collectible items with clear visual distinction (milk bottles, hearts, bells). The game uses a whimsical visual language (baby protagonist, cake levels, rattle weapon) to offset its core curse narrative. The magical rattle serves as primary visual marker of player agency and fairy protection. Difficulty modifications (Easy/Normal modes in Famicom release) adjust enemy damage and starting health, altering danger signal intensity without changing visual communication. The game lacks explicit faction indicators beyond protagonist vs. enemies; world-state is communicated through absence (missing babies) and presence (hostile creatures). FDS to Famicom port retained visual communication but remixed audio to compensate for hardware limitations.
